title: Design occupations n.e.c.
description: Design, develop, test, and evaluate integrated systems for managing industrial production processes, including human work factors, quality control, inventory control, logistics and material flow, cost analysis, and production coordination. Design objects, facilities, and environments to optimize human well-being and overall system performance, applying theory, principles, and data regarding the relationship between humans and respective technology. Investigate and analyze characteristics of human behavior and performance as it relates to the use of technology. Design or plan protocols for equipment or processes to produce products meeting internal and external purity, safety, and quality requirements. (Based on 27 related US occupations.)
